english: Salmon Teriyaki Rice Bowl with Beef
japanese: 照り焼きサーモン牛丼

food_subgroup: Mixed fish and meat rice bowl (donburi)

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for teriyaki salmon and beef rice bowl. FOODON_00002809 (rice bowl food product) is the closest defensible parent category.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 does not have a specific code for teriyaki salmon and beef donburi. A0FJP (fish and meat mixed dish) is used as the closest parent. Exact match unavailable.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No suitable SNOMED CT concept exists for this specific Japanese mixed rice bowl preparation.

main_ingredients: teriyaki sauce (soy sauce, mirin, sake, sugar)

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is approximately 450 g, comprising a bed of steamed white rice topped with a teriyaki-glazed salmon fillet and simmered beef, as served at Sukiya Singapore.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — Salmon, Atlantic, farmed, cooked, dry heat (FDC ID 175168); Beef, short loin, top loin steak, separable lean only, trimmed to 0" fat, all grades, cooked, broiled (FDC ID 174032); White rice, cooked (FDC ID 168880); Teriyaki sauce (FDC ID 172237) — composite estimation
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service, FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No single approved-source entry exists for this specific Sukiya menu item. Nutrient values are estimated by weighted composite of USDA FoodData Central entries for cooked Atlantic salmon (approx. 25% by weight), simmered lean beef (approx. 15% by weight), cooked white rice (approx. 55% by weight), and teriyaki sauce (approx. 5% by weight), scaled to per-100 g basis. Serving size estimated at 450 g based on comparable Japanese donburi portions. Confidence is medium due to composite estimation.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson FS et al.; white rice cooked GI reference value 64 (glucose=100); teriyaki sauce minor contribution; composite bowl GI estimated from rice-dominant component
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for this specific Sukiya teriyaki salmon and beef bowl. GI of 64 is estimated from the dominant carbohydrate component: cooked white rice (GI 64, glucose reference, per University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2021 international tables). Protein and fat from salmon and beef will modestly attenuate postprandial glycaemic response in practice. Glycemic load calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (64 × 87.75) / 100 ≈ 56.2, where carbohydrate per serving = 19.5 g/100g × 450 g = 87.75 g.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content from teriyaki sauce and soy-based simmered beef
hypertension.guidance: Each 450 g serving provides approximately 1395 mg sodium, representing 58–70% of the recommended daily limit of 2000–2400 mg for hypertensive individuals; those managing blood pressure should request reduced-sauce portions or limit frequency of consumption.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High glycemic load from white rice base combined with added sugars in teriyaki glaze
type2_diabetes.guidance: The estimated glycemic load of approximately 56 per serving is high for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes; portion control of the rice component and pairing with additional non-starchy vegetables is recommended to moderate postprandial blood glucose excursions.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Omega-3 fatty acids from salmon offset by moderate sodium from teriyaki sauce
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Salmon provides cardioprotective long-chain omega-3 fatty acids (EPA and DHA), which support favourable lipid profiles; however, the elevated sodium content from teriyaki sauce warrants attention for individuals with cardiovascular disease, and reduced-sodium soy sauce variants should be preferred where available.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Teriyaki Salmon Bowl with Beef (照り焼きサーモン牛丼, Teriyaki Sāmon Gyūdon) is a Japanese donburi-style rice bowl dish served at Sukiya, a Japanese fast-casual chain operating in Singapore. The dish features a bed of steamed Japanese short-grain white rice topped with a grilled Atlantic salmon fillet glazed with teriyaki sauce — a sweet-savoury mixture of soy sauce, mirin, sake, and sugar — alongside Sukiya's signature simmered gyudon-style beef, which is slow-cooked in a dashi-based broth with onions, soy sauce, and mirin. The combination delivers a contrast of textures: the salmon is lightly charred on the exterior with a moist, flaky interior, while the beef is tender and richly flavoured. Garnishes typically include sesame seeds and sliced spring onion. Nutritionally, the dish is a moderate-calorie, protein-rich meal providing approximately 148 kcal per 100 g, with notable contributions of omega-3 fatty acids from salmon, complete protein from both fish and beef, and B-vitamins including B12 and niacin. The white rice base gives the dish a medium glycemic index of approximately 64, resulting in a high glycemic load per serving (~56 GL units) that is clinically relevant for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes or insulin resistance. Sodium content is elevated due to the teriyaki glaze and simmered beef sauce, making this a moderate concern for hypertensive individuals. The dish is dairy-free and suitable for pescatarian-adjacent diets, though it contains soy, wheat (from soy sauce), fish, and sesame allergens. It is not halal-certified in its standard form due to the use of mirin (rice wine). Priced at SGD 17.40 at Sukiya Funan, Singapore.

How much protein is in a teriyaki salmon bowl?

confidence_reason: No single approved-source entry exists for this specific Sukiya menu item. Nutrient values are estimated by weighted composite of USDA FoodData Central entries for the constituent ingredients (cooked Atlantic salmon, simmered lean beef, cooked white rice, teriyaki sauce). GI is estimated from the dominant carbohydrate component (white rice, GI 64) per University of Sydney GI Database. Confidence is medium due to composite estimation and absence of direct laboratory analysis or HPB/USDA entry for this exact dish.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are composite estimates derived from USDA FoodData Central ingredient-level entries; no direct laboratory analysis of this Sukiya menu item was available from any approved source. 2. Serving size of 450 g is estimated based on comparable Japanese donburi portions; actual Sukiya portion may vary. 3. trans_fat_g is reported as 0.0; this is confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central data for the constituent ingredients (salmon, lean beef, rice) with no industrial trans-fat sources present. 4. monounsaturated_fat_g (1.1), polyunsaturated_fat_g (1.0) are estimated from USDA ingredient data. 5. GI of 64 is an analogous estimate based on white rice as the dominant glycemic carbohydrate source; actual GI of the complete dish may be modestly lower due to protein and fat co-ingestion attenuating glycaemic response. 6. The dish is not halal-certified in its standard form as mirin (rice wine) is used in the teriyaki sauce and simmered beef preparation. 7. Allergens include fish (salmon), soy, wheat (from soy sauce), and sesame. 8. FoodOn URI FOODON_00002809 and FoodEx2 code A0FJP are used as closest parent matches; no exact ontology codes exist for this specific dish. 9. All -1 sentinel values in per_100g have been replaced with estimated numeric values from USDA FoodData Central composite data where defensible; no -1 values remain in this record.
